title: Nervilia scottii
taxonomy: Nervilia scottii is a species of orchid. It is classified within the family Orchidaceae. Nervilia scottii is further classified in the subfamily Vanilloideae. This species is native to tropical Africa and belongs to the genus Nervilia. The classification of Nervilia scottii is used to distinguish it from other species within the same genus.

intro: Nervilia scottii, also known as Scott's Nervilia, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Nervilia scottii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Nervilia scottii is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems.
